rulings.Case Title: GORDON EATON VS. C.C. MOORE & CO. ENGINEERS
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 21, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Warren Pumps, LLC¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff failed to present admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are sustained in part and overruled in part: The statements in plaintiff¿s declaration explaining how he knew the Warren pumps at issue were new based on visual cues are admissible. The statements in plaintiff¿s declaration to show the pumps were new based on seeing paperwork are inadmissible. Plaintiff¿s opinions regarding the asbestos content of the gaskets and packing at issue are inadmissible. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Fening¿s declaration are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: RANDALL WILKINS VS. HAMILTON MATERIALS, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 21, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant H&C Investment Associates, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff¿s claim for punitive damages is off calendar. The motion for summary judgment is denied because defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that any exposure to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant was not a substantial factor in causing or contributing to the development of plaintiff¿s asbestos-related disease. The declaration of Mr. Scoby is inadmissible because he demonstrates no foundation to state that any asbestos in a Childers product would have been encapsulated. The declarations of Dr. Rabinovitz and Dr. Sandler do not negate an element of plaintiff¿s claims because those declarations are largely inadmissible and assume, without foundation and based on Mr. Scoby¿s inadmissible declaration, that any asbestos that would have been released from plaintiff¿s removal, rasping and/or disturbance of the Childers products would have been encapsulated. The court considered plaintiff¿s written objections and, further, nothing prohibits plaintiff from making oral objections at the hearing. Plaintiff¿s objections to the declarations of Mr. Scoby, Dr. Rabinovitz and Dr. Sandler and to defendant¿s responses to standard interrogatories are sustained. Plaintiff has dismissed his claim for punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: RANDALL WILKINS VS. HAMILTON MATERIALS, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 21, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Limbach Facility Services, LLC¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1-2 (exposure/causation) and off calendar as to issues 3 and 4 (strict liability and punitive damages). Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense applies to bar plaintiff¿s claims against it. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff¿s claims are barred by the Privette doctrine. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that it owed no duty to plaintiff just because the asbestos-containing products and/or materials that its employees used, disturbed and/or installed in plaintiff¿s presence were not manufactured or installed by defendant (fireproofing) or were provided by plaintiff¿s employer (insulation inserts). Plaintiff has dismissed his strict liability cause of action and claim for punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD KEELING VS. C.C. MOORE & CO. ENGINEERS
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 21, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Defendant Pneumo Abex LLC¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether exposure to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his asbestos-related illness. The court will note that defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess evidence that he was exposed to a product attributable to defendant. Even if Mr. Indelicato¿s declaration is considered, when read in conjunction with plaintiff¿s deposition testimony it merely creates a triable issue whether the brake linings at issue were manufactured, sold and/or supplied by Abex. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
